title: Bug: Constraint Error: Duplicate key during data insert (1.2.0)
description:
### What happens?

DuckDB v1.2.0 has announced that [Over-Eager Unique Constraint Checking](https://duckdb.org/2025/02/05/announcing-duckdb-120.html#indexing) issue has been resolved and it is now possible to drop the data and insert new rows with the same keys.

I still see `Constraint Error: Duplicate key during data insert` error using duckdb v1.2.0 when deleting and inserting data in the same transaction. 

### To Reproduce

Run the following script, that removes all records from the table and then re-insert them as part of the same transaction resulting in primary key constraint violation error, where it should not.

`./load_lineitem.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash

DB_FILE="./tpch_lineitem.db"
rm -f $DB_FILE

duckdb --version

duckdb $DB_FILE <<EOF

-- Create test table with primary key and index
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS lineitem(l_orderkey INTEGER, l_partkey INTEGER, l_suppkey INTEGER, l_linenumber INTEGER, l_quantity DECIMAL(15,2), l_extendedprice DECIMAL(15,2), l_discount DECIMAL(15,2), l_tax DECIMAL(15,2), l_returnflag VARCHAR, l_linestatus VARCHAR, l_shipdate DATE, l_commitdate DATE, l_receiptdate DATE, l_shipinstruct VARCHAR, l_shipmode VARCHAR, l_comment VARCHAR, PRIMARY KEY (l_linenumber, l_orderkey));
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "i_lineitem_l_shipdate" ON "lineitem" ("l_shipdate");

-- Insert initial data from S3 Parquet
INSERT INTO lineitem 
SELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://spiceai-demo-datasets/tpch/lineitem/*.parquet') LIMIT 1000;

SELECT COUNT(*) as initial_insert_count FROM lineitem;

# -- Begin transaction: delete existing data and reinsert
BEGIN;
DELETE FROM lineitem;
SELECT COUNT(*) as count_after_drop FROM lineitem;
INSERT INTO lineitem 
SELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://spiceai-demo-datasets/tpch/lineitem/*.parquet') LIMIT 1000;
SELECT COUNT(*) as count_after_reinsert FROM lineitem;
COMMIT;

EOF

echo "Overwrite attempt completed."
```

key_bug_features: Duplicate key error during data reinsert in transaction
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders(order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, order_date DATE, total_amount DECIMAL(10,2));

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_items(order_item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, order_id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, quantity INTEGER, price DECIMAL(10,2), FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders(order_id));
